sections.title: Six tools. One substrate.
sections.lede: Every PlausiDen tool shares the same typed contracts. Compose what you need; ignore what you don't.

background.alt: Wide editorial photograph evoking infrastructure + scale

sections.text: Six tools. Use one. Or all six. Every tool can be swapped, rewired, or replaced because the contract between them is typed, not configured.

sections.caption: The six tools in PlausiDen — composable, not glued.

items.definition: Typed build pipeline. 27 audit phases per commit, Merkle-chained signed reports, deterministic output. The same input compiles to the same bytes — today, next decade, forever.
Typed UI primitives + 14 themes + OKLCH palette + dual-theme by default + WCAG 2.1 AA gated at build time. Every CmsSection variant is a closed enum; raw class strings fail lint.

Runtime auditor walking the real DOM. 120+ typed detectors no static analysis can run — contrast across translucent stacks, layout collapse at narrow viewports, gradient-text mid-character clipping, phantom focus on off-screen elements.

Operator capture. Typed event stream, lossless replay, local-first storage, opt-in by user. Replays the exact session — keystrokes, scroll, focus, latency — without ever leaving the visitor's browser.
Content as typed data. JSON files in version control. No database, no template language. Schema migrations are minutes — the types ARE the migration.

AI evaluation, not generation. The Critic trait gates every proposal at a compiler-enforced seam. LLMs are constrained candidate generators; the Critic is the brain. LLM-pluggable, optional, sandboxable.

sections.caption: The Forge build pipeline — typed contracts at every seam.

sections.text: Forge runs 27 audit phases on every commit. Loom ships the typed primitive count via a renderer-injected fact — currently:
Six deploy networks ship today (clearnet, Tor, I2P, IPFS, Gemini, Lokinet) — the same Forge build produces every variant from the same input.

sections.heading: How a build runs
sections.lede: Every commit traces through the same pipeline.

items.title: Parse CMS
items.body: Read cms/*.json. Closed-enum CmsSection variants with deny_unknown_fields catch malformed input at the boundary.

items.title: Validate
items.body: 27 audit phases — tokens, CSP, a11y landmarks, semantic HTML, theme contrast, path consistency, perf budget.

items.title: Render
items.body: loom-cms-render walks the typed page, emits semantic HTML + the Loom skin. No template language.

items.title: Sign + chain
items.body: Build report hashed into the Merkle chain. Ed25519 signature. Independently verifiable.

rows.label: Multi-network publish
values: Tor / I2P / IPFS / Gemini / Lokinet / clearnet

items.title: Forge 0.18 — typed CMS pipeline
items.body: 27 audit phases + SLSA-attested SBOMs + Multi-network DeployAdapter.

items.title: Loom 0.4 — 142 typed primitives
items.body: Catalogue: Layout / Editorial / Marketing / Media / Commerce / Social / Forms / Navigation / Feedback / Auth + Crucible widget.

items.title: forge-auth + Crucible
items.body: Passwordless / WebAuthn / MFA via typed AuthProvider.

items.title: LFI-augmented Critic
items.body: Neurosymbolic substrate via the Critic trait. Drop-in via cargo --features lfi.

items.question: Can I use only one tool?
answer: Yes. Forge is the build pipeline; everything else is opt-in.
Common starting point: Forge + Loom. Add Crawler when you want runtime audits. Add Crucible + forge-auth when you ship logins.

items.question: How does the LFI integration work?
answer: forge-critic ships the Critic trait + NoopCritic + LlmCritic in every build. The LFI-backed Critic is gated behind cargo --features lfi.
Bring your own LLM provider. The platform never sees your prompts.

items.question: Does it deploy to Tor / I2P / IPFS?
answer: Yes. Six DeployAdapter implementations: clearnet, Tor onion, I2P eepsite, IPFS/IPNS, Gemini, Lokinet.

sections.title: Use one. Or all six.
sections.lede: PlausiDen is opinionated about types and unopinionated about adoption.
